civillis_entities.configuration.enableCE: Master switch to enable/disable entire CEA mod functionality. When disabled: No sampling, no marker processing, no particle effects. This has HIGHEST priority - overrides all other settings when false
civillis_entities.configuration.manualPl: Manual Placement Mode - for admin/custom map creation. When disabled (default): Automatic mode - heat decays normally, markers auto-create/update/delete. When enabled: Manual mode - stops all automatic marker management and heat decay. Use with /ce create marker and /ce remove marker for full manual control. Markers become permanent and only change via admin commands
civillis_entities.configuration.entityWe: List of civilization entities with weights for heatmap system. Format: 'namespace:entity_type:weight' (e.g., 'minecraft:villager:1.0'). If weight is omitted, default value 2.0 will be used. Only entities in this list will be tracked and contribute to settlement markers. Examples: ['minecraft:villager'] (uses default weight 2.0), ['minecraft:villager:1.5', 'minecraft:iron_golem:3.0'] (custom weights)
civillis_entities.configuration.enablePa: Enable particle effects when scanning entities with Civillis tools
civillis_entities.configuration.particle: Duration of particle effect (in ticks, 20 ticks = 1 second)
civillis_entities.configuration.initialP: Particle type for initial burst effect. Available types: HAPPY_VILLAGER, GLOW, HEART, FLAME, SMOKE, etc.
civillis_entities.configuration.continuo: Particle type for continuous effect. Available types: GLOW, SPELL, INSTANT_SPELL, WITCH, etc.
Interval between continuous particle spawns (in ticks). Lower values = more frequent particles (more performance impact)
civillis_entities.configuration.enableVe: Enable verbose logging for CEA operations. When disabled, only warnings and errors will be logged. When enabled, detailed info about sampling, processing, and entity tracking will be logged
civillis_entities.configuration.usePerce: Heatmap decay mode selector. false = Fixed-value decay (default): Subtract a fixed amount each sampling; true = Percentage decay: Reduce heat by a percentage each sampling. Fixed decay is more stable for low-heat areas; Percentage decay encourages spreading out settlements
civillis_entities.configuration.heatDeca: Fixed decay amount per sampling period (used when usePercentageDecay = false). Higher values make heat harder to accumulate, requiring more entities or frequent sampling
Percentage decay rate per sampling period (used when usePercentageDecay = true). Value between 0.0 and 1.0 (e.g., 0.10 = 10% decay). Higher values make heat harder to accumulate in high-heat areas
civillis_entities.configuration.useLinea: Level calculation mode selector. false = Curve-based calculation (default): Higher levels require more heat (slower progression); true = Linear calculation: level = heat / threshold (simple and predictable). Curve mode makes it harder to reach high levels, encouraging more settlement activity
civillis_entities.configuration.levelCur: Exponent for curve-based level calculation (used when useLinearLevelCalculation = false). Formula: level = (heat / threshold) ^ (1/exponent). Higher values = slower progression at high levels (more challenging); Lower values = faster progression (easier to reach high levels). Recommended range: 2.0-3.5 (default: 2.5 for balanced difficulty). Examples: 2.0 = square root (easy), 2.5 = moderate, 3.0 = cube root (hard)
